raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1053 bans the distribution of plastic bags at grocery store checkouts in California.",
    "The loophole in California's original plastic bag ban allowed thicker plastic bags to be offered despite the ban on single-use plastic bags.",
    "Plastic bags are one of the most common types of litter found in California’s waterways, parks, and communities.",
    "According to the United Nations Environment Program, up to five trillion plastic bags are used worldwide every year.",
    "Plastic bags are used for an average of just 12 minutes before being discarded, but their environmental impact can last for centuries.",
    "SB 1053 is expected to help prevent further pollution and protect California's environment for future generations.",
    "Under the new law, stores will only be able to offer recycled paper bags or reusable bags brought by consumers.",
    "Plastic grocery bags have ranked in the top ten most commonly collected items by California Coastal Cleanup Day volunteers in seven of the last 10 years.",
    "Over the last decade, volunteers have cleaned up enough plastic bags to span the length of the Golden Gate Bridge nearly 30 times.",
    "Plastic grocery bags are one of the top five deadliest forms of plastic pollution to marine life.",
    "Banning plastic grocery bags has been shown to reduce this type of plastic pollution.",
    "Plastic bags break into microplastics that contaminate drinking water and threaten health."
  ]
}
```
